content.problemStatement:
Given two arrays, find their union. The union of two arrays should contain all the distinct elements present in both arrays.


Input Format

The first line contains two integers, NNN and MMM, representing the sizes of the first and second arrays, respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers, representing the elements of the first array.
The third line contains MMM space-separated integers, representing the elements of the second array.


Output Format
Print the distinct elements of the union of the two arrays in ascending order, separated by spaces.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two integers N and M (0 ≤ N, M ≤ 15) – the sizes of the first and second arrays. The second line contains N space‑separated integers (may be empty if N = 0). The third line contains M space‑separated integers (may be empty if M = 0).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the distinct elements of the union of the two arrays in ascending order, separated by a single space. Print a newline after each test case. If the union is empty, output an empty line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Both arrays are read, duplicates (1 and 2) are removed, the remaining numbers are sorted and printed.
No common elements, so the union consists of all numbers in ascending order.
The first array is empty; the union equals the second array, which is already sorted.

solution.approach: For each test case read both arrays, insert every element into a hash set (or std::unordered_set) to automatically discard duplicates. After all elements are inserted, copy the set into a vector/array, sort it in ascending order and print the numbers separated by spaces. The set guarantees uniqueness, sorting provides the required order. The algorithm runs in O((N+M) log(N+M)) time per test case and uses O(N+M) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O((N+M) log(N+M)) per test case, where N and M are the sizes of the two arrays.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N+M) for the set storing the union elements.

code.Python3:
import sys
def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
    if not data:
        return
    idx = 0
    T = int(data[idx].strip()); idx+=1
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        while idx < len(data) and data[idx]=='' : idx+=1
        N, M = map(int, data[idx].split()); idx+=1
        arr1 = []
        if N>0:
            arr1 = list(map(int, data[idx].split()))
            idx+=1
        else:
            if idx < len(data) and data[idx].strip()=='': idx+=1
        arr2 = []
        if M>0:
            arr2 = list(map(int, data[idx].split()))
            idx+=1
        else:
            if idx < len(data) and data[idx].strip()=='': idx+=1
        union_set = set(arr1)
        union_set.update(arr2)
        out_lines.append(' '.join(map(str, sorted(union_set))))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))
if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
